items.front: What sources are acceptable for current altimeter settings?
items.back: Use direct-reading instruments or reports obtained directly from weather reporting stations. If a pilot wants millibars, get the equivalent from the nearest reporting station.

items.front: Below the lowest usable flight level, how must altimeter settings be issued?
items.back: Terminal identifies the source if it is for another location; en route identifies every source. Issue them to departures, arrivals, en route aircraft, and before descent below the lowest usable flight level.

items.front: What high-barometric-pressure restriction applies when altimeter setting exceeds 31.00 inches?
items.back: Advise high barometric pressure procedures are in effect. Arrivals and en route aircraft keep 31.00 set until the final approach fix; departures set 31.00 before the lower of a crossing altitude or 1,500 AGL.
